message:
fix: Thrift compact protocol list type ID (9, not 11)

THRIFT_LIST was set to 11 (map type) instead of 9 (list type) in the
compact protocol. This caused skip() to silently fail on list-typed
fields like encoding_stats (field 13 in ColumnMetaData), misaligning
the Thrift reader for all subsequent fields.

Snappy Parquet files have encoding_stats that plain files don't, which
is why only Snappy parsing was broken (0 rows, missing columns, wrong
dtypes).

Also adds SET (10) and MAP (11) handling to skip() for completeness,
and fixture-based tests for plain, snappy, default, and 100k Parquet.
